name: 广州
description: Guangzhou, the capital of Guangdong province, is a historic trading port and the birthplace of Cantonese culture. Known as 'Canton', it offers world-renowned dim sum, fascinating food markets, and the iconic Canton Tower. The city blends Southern Chinese traditions with modern architecture, and serves as the gateway to Hong Kong and Macau.
highlights: Chen Clan Ancestral Hall - Traditional Cantonese architecture
Beijing Rd Pedestrian Street - Historic shopping district
Baiyun Mountain - City views and tea houses

climate.tips: Best visited in autumn and early spring. Summers are hot, humid, and rainy (May-September). Typhoon season is July-September. Light clothing most of the year. Mosquitoes common in summer.

attractions.name: 广州塔
attractions.description: The iconic TV tower with a unique 'fish belly' design, offering panoramic city views and the world's highest Skywalk.
attractions.tips: Book evening tickets for best views and light show. Sunset timing is magical. Lower decks less crowded.

attractions.name: 陈家祠
attractions.description: A meticulously preserved traditional Cantonese building complex with intricate woodcarvings and stone sculptures.
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekdays. Excellent traditional crafts inside. Combined with Shamian Island walk.

attractions.name: 沙面
attractions.description: A former foreign concession with beautiful European architecture, tree-lined streets, and colonial-era buildings.
attractions.tips: Best in afternoon for photos. Combine with nearby Huangshatang food street. Great for wedding photos.

attractions.name: 白云山
attractions.description: A city park with peaks offering respite from the urban bustle and panoramic views of Guangzhou.
attractions.tips: Take the cable car up, walk down for different views. Bring water and snacks. Morning visits cooler.

attractions.name: 珠江夜游
attractions.description: A scenic cruise along the Pearl River showcasing Guangzhou's illuminated skyline and historic bridges.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge. Outdoor decks colder in winter. Best in clear weather.

attractions.name: 六榕寺
attractions.description: A famous Buddhist temple dating from 537 AD with an iconic 9-story pagoda and ancient banyan tree.
attractions.tips: One of Guangzhou's oldest temples. The pagoda offers views over the city. Best in morning for fewer worshippers.

attractions.name: 广州大剧院
attractions.description: Zaha Hadid's masterpiece of curved concrete and glass on the Pearl River, a world-class performing arts venue.
attractions.tips: Best photographed from across the river at night. Book a show for the full experience. Combine with Canton Tower visit.

attractions.name: 东山口
attractions.description: Historic Cantonese neighborhood with European-style villas, art galleries, trendy cafes, and tree-lined streets.
attractions.tips: Best explored on foot. Great for brunch and coffee. Combine with nearby Taojiang Rd for art galleries.

attractions.name: 海珠湿地公园
attractions.description: A beautiful wetland park in the heart of the city with water channels, lotus ponds, and bird-watching opportunities.
attractions.tips: Best in lotus season (June-August). Wear comfortable shoes for walking. Great escape from city bustle.

attractions.name: 黄埔古港
attractions.description: The historic departure point of China's maritime Silk Road, with traditional architecture and Cantonese food stalls.
attractions.tips: Try the famous ginger milk pudding (姜撞奶) here. Combine with nearby Whampoa Military Academy.

attractions.name: 越秀公园
attractions.description: Guangzhou's largest park featuring the ancient Zhenhai Tower, beautiful gardens, and the iconic Five Goat Statue.
attractions.tips: Start here for history and city views. Take the cable car for panoramic views. Best in early morning.

restaurants.name: 御宝轩
restaurants.address: IFC Mall, 5 Zhujiang West Rd, Tianhe District
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star Cantonese fine dining with exceptional dim sum and sophisticated ambiance.

restaurants.name: 江- by Chef Fei
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star restaurant by celebrity Chef Fei with innovative take on classic Cantonese dishes.

restaurants.name: 利苑酒家
restaurants.address: Various locations including Tianhe and Zhujiang New Town
restaurants.description: Two diamond Black Pearl with legendary dim sum and consistent quality over decades.

restaurants.name: 陶陶居
restaurants.address: Various locations including Beijing Rd and Shangxiajiu
restaurants.description: Legendary Cantonese restaurant established in 1880, famous for dim sum and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 点都德
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving authentic dim sum in modern surroundings.

restaurants.name: 白天鹅宾馆
restaurants.description: Two diamond Black Pearl in historic hotel, serving Cantonese dim sum with Shamian Island views.

restaurants.name: 莲香楼
restaurants.description: Legendary Cantonese teahouse established in 1889, famous for mooncakes and dim sum.

restaurants.name: 惠食佳
restaurants.description: One diamond Black Pearl famous for clay pot cooking and high-quality fresh dishes.

restaurants.name: 虾饺妹
restaurants.description: Popular casual dim sum chain with fresh, quality ingredients at reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: 广州酒家
restaurants.description: Popular Cantonese restaurant chain established in 1935, known for quality dim sum and banquet dishes.

arrival.tips: Second busiest airport in China. Metro Line 3 connects to city center. High-speed rail station nearby.

arrival.to: Guangzhou South Railway Station / Guangzhou Railway Station
arrival.tips: High-speed rail very convenient. Guangzhou South is for high-speed, Guangzhou for conventional trains.

metro: Single ride: 2-16 CNY depending on distance
Line 3 very crowded during peak hours
bus: Extensive network but complex to navigate
Buses marked with numbers and pinyin
taxi: Didi app fully English-friendly in Guangzhou
bike: Unlock: 1 CNY, then 0.15 CNY per 15 min

hotels.name: 广州四季酒店
hotels.address: 3F, IFZ Tower 2, 5 Zhujiang West Rd, Tianhe
hotels.bookingTips: Request tower-view rooms. Very popular for special occasions.

hotels.name: 广州香格里拉大酒店
hotels.address: 1 Hua Cheng Da Dao, Tianhe District
hotels.bookingTips: Riverside rooms worth the extra cost.

hotels.name: 广州白天鹅宾馆
hotels.bookingTips: Request rooms with river views. Famous for breakfast dim sum.

payment.description: Dominant payment in Guangzhou, accepted everywhere
tips: Guangzhou is modern - foreign cards work at many places
Even locals prefer Alipay for larger payments

payment.description: Still useful for traditional markets and small vendors

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Food Culture
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou is the birthplace of dim sum. Try morning tea (yum cha) culture - it's a social ritual. Fresh and light flavors are prized.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese vs Mandarin
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou locals speak Cantonese (广东话) natively, though Mandarin is also understood. English more commonly spoken here than in northern cities.

culturalTips.title: Humid Weather Adaptation
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou is very humid year-round. Stay hydrated, use sun protection, and acclimatize to the warmth.

culturalTips.title: Cantonese Breakfast
culturalTips.content: Guangzhou locals enjoy '早茶' (morning tea) as a social ritual. Arrive at dim sum restaurants around 9-10am. It's normal to spend 2+ hours finishing multiple courses of dumplings, buns, and rice rolls.

emergencyContacts.name: 中山大学附属第一医院
emergencyContacts.nameEn: First Affiliated Hospital of Sun Yat-sen University

emergencyContacts.name: 美国驻广州总领事馆
emergencyContacts.address: 26th Floor, Maybin Tower, 99 Linhe Rd, Tianhe

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa. 144h transit visa available.
quickFacts.water: Tap water boiled or use bottled water
